metadata.title: Build a PreToolUse Hook That Blocks Grade Generation with Claude Code

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: I want to actually build the PreToolUse hook that stops Claude from ever writing a grade into a student file, walked through end to end. Then: (1) help me describe every grade pattern to block, a letter with a plus or minus, and any 'overall performance' sentence followed by a grade; (2) have Claude draft the stdin-reading shell script that exits nonzero on a match; (3) show me how to register it in settings.json and test that a real Write actually gets blocked.

props.command: I want to actually build the PreToolUse hook that stops Claude from ever writing a grade into a student file, walked through end to end. Then: (1) help me describe every grade pattern to block, a letter with a plus or minus, and any 'overall performance' sentence followed by a grade; (2) have Claude draft the stdin-reading shell script that exits nonzero on a match; (3) show me how to register it in settings.json and test that a real Write actually gets blocked.
props.segment: Build a PreToolUse Hook That Blocks

beats.narration: NEVER generate a final grade in CLAUDE.md lasts two sessions. A PreToolUse hook lasts forever. Here is the twenty-line script that turns do not into cannot.

A CLAUDE.md instruction is probabilistic — Claude weights it and sometimes overrides it. A hook is deterministic — it executes mechanically regardless of prompt phrasing. The teacher gets reliability, not compliance.
Ask Claude to write the PreToolUse hook script and the settings.json configuration.

props.command:
claude "Write PreToolUse hook block-grades.sh:
  read Write tool input from stdin as JSON
  extract 'content' field
  grep -qE for grade patterns:
    [0-9]{1,3}%, [A-F][+-]?,
    [0-9]+/[0-9]+
  exit 1 + 'BLOCKED: contains final grade'
  Also write settings.json config."

beats.narration: The hook reads the Write tool input from stdin as JSON, extracts the content field, and runs a regex against grade patterns — percentages, letter grades, and fraction scores.

props.code:
#!/bin/bash
# block-grades.sh — PreToolUse hook
input=$(cat)  # JSON from stdin
content=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c \
  "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('content',''))")

if echo "$content" | grep -qE \
  '[0-9]{1,3}%|[A-F][+-]?[[:space:]]|[0-9]+/[0-9]+|[0-9]+ out of [0-9]+';
then
  echo "BLOCKED: content contains a final grade" >&2
  exit 1
fi
exit 0

beats.narration: Hook test: ask Claude to summarize student performance. Claude generates Overall performance: B+. The hook reads the proposed Write input, detects B+, exits 1. Claude receives the block message and regenerates without the grade. The grade never reached the file.
Extend the hook to distinguish grade percentages from quantity percentages.

props.command:
claude "Extend hook to also detect
  grade patterns in percentile ranges:
  'in the top 15%' vs '85%'
  distinguish quantity from grade.
Update grep pattern."

beats.narration: Extended hook: the student scored in the top 15% of the class — not blocked, it is a quantity. The student earned 85% — blocked, it is a grade. The pattern correctly distinguishes context by checking for grade-adjacent language rather than bare percentages.

The PreToolUse hook is the enforcement layer below the instruction layer. Write the instruction in CLAUDE.md for transparency — wire the hook in settings.json for reliability. The two layers serve different purposes and both are needed.
Next: deploy a pattern-analysis subagent that keeps the main session context clean.
Like and subscribe for more Claude Code for Teachers.
